mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding a 0.5 percentage point increase over a three-month window signals an abrupt labor market shock, triggering risk-off repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the trigger yields a negative predictive value below 0.40 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in housing starts and commercial real estate lending (RSAFS) indicates a synchronized drag on aggregate demand, which reduces corporate revenue growth expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a negative Rank IC greater than -0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns in joint contraction periods do not statistically differ from baseline (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production exceeds its 10-year trend while inflation remains anchored below 2.5%, supply-side efficiency gains translate directly into higher corporate margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in this regime are not significantly positive relative to the full sample mean, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP exceeding 2.0% annualized rate confirms robust economic momentum, which sustains earnings revisions upward and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis yields a positive predictive value below 0.50 over a 5-year walk-forward test, or the correlation between GDP growth surprises and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 2.0%, the cost of capital rises sufficiently to suppress equity valuations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days as discount rates adjust.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following the threshold breach are not statistically negative (t-stat > -1.65), or the signal's information ratio remains below 0.20 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate indicates structural labor market friction, which increases wage-push inflation pressures and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model fit (AIC reduction < 2) or produces a Rank IC above -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, indicating no incremental predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases where housing starts accelerate by more than 10% quarter-over-quarter, consumer confidence rebounds rapidly, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns in identified recovery windows are not significantly positive relative to non-recovery periods (p > 0.05), or the trigger identifies fewer than 10 valid episodes over a 20-year sample.
